It was a fun game but so many heroes in played may the whole experience of selecting the two heroes way more complicated.

Fun setup. I was able to use wiccan's common to keep ultimate spiderman from bricking. Only reason it wasn't a 5 for fun was Maria's Declare martial law filled the city right before double enter cards and caused most of my escapes.

column36: Found n setup. Wish I had more time to make second attempt as had some bad luck early with villain deck draws. Also, man I hate Maria Hill clogging our decks with worthless Shield Agents. Rarely saw the specials.

We did quite well in our first game, won in only 23 turns, but had very few points and still had one escape, so we decided to submit the second attempt; interested to see if we chose poorly! We chose Cap Marvel due to her +1 for each Strength card, but never got that card in hand!  But we were still pretty pleased with our hero choices, worked well.

Maria Hill did the Maria Hill thing and Maria Hilled all over the place. Felt like a Sisyphean task trying to keep the city clean, clogging up our decks in the process. Probably should have leaned more into KO as opposed to card draw, but oh well.

I leaned into the theme and knew that might not be optimal.  I was close to winning on attempt one, but had 4 escapes.  I forgot how much Maria Hill filled the city in her tactics.  I feel like I rarely get to use Civil War in these tournaments, so that was fun.  I always forget how much split cards slow down each turn's decision making process.

First round, everything went sideways and lost on the 7th twist. 2nd round, switched in Legion and Lightshow dominated. PE worked like an energy cannon.

Mojo is always a hard spineless one to defeat with his knack for shielding himself. 
First time playing the mixtape. I really enjoyed the variety of the enemies with no repeats aside from the henchmen, I didn't like that it led to so many rares getting KOed. 
First time with Part Thor. I built a nice recruiting deck on the right, but it the overcommitment on recruit led to some escapes.
Left built a decent Covert / Lightshow deck and that went pretty alright delivering most of the damage, but it did get pretty big.

column36: It was a bit of a nail biter but in  good way. I was not sure I was going to make it with all the scheme twists. And I KO'd one of my own cards before realizing that was going to really work against me. Great scenario!

SO MANY CARDS! :D What a crazy scheme and setup to sample a lot of cards! Haha Used King Black Panther and White Wolf for their Ambush abilities with all the HQ turnover from the scheme. They also gave nice hero class overlap with the other heroes, particularly Black Panther's multiple colors. Thor didn't do anything for me, but I found nice synergies elsewhere. All the villain and mastermind wounding was great for managing the board over multiple turns. Was really tempted to try Valkyrie because her rare seems absolutely nuts with the scheme. I figured with 7 heroes and a lot of HQ KO's that it would be hard to recruit her one rare card though, and her other cards didn't feel as synergistic. It was quite the collection management to pull all the pieces for this setup, but it was worth it! My first play was enjoyable but not as fun. Had a less-friendly villain deck shuffle and couldn't quite keep up with the scheme twists. Great way to start the tourney!
I like the scheme.

First setup I couldn’t get anything to work for me. Second attempt I changed it up by using Captain Marvel and Crystal. Blue and Green on one side and a color combo on the other hand and ignored the tech cards from Beast and it worked much better.

After losing the first game on the seventh scheme (well, six and a master strike), I decided to try Phoenix to try to mitigate the KOed colored heroes and maybe get one more strike in.  My plan was to only recruit the uncommon that lets you grab a hero KOed this turn and let the ones that KO colored heroes just sit and get KOed on the next scheme.  I got lucky and was able to recruit two of them, and was actually able to use one (doubled up with a Prodigy Special Sidekick) to pull out two KOed heroes.  But that wasn't enough.  One for Scheme Twist and it was going to be over -- sure with only 33 KOed Heroes instead of 35 -- but wouldn't have made any difference.  Don't think this method would likely ever get you the four you would need.

This setup up was an abundance of hero options so I just went with two heroes I enjoy playing. I had never used this scheme before but it is definitely became one of my favorites. I would have given the set up a 5 if it wasn't for the AP I had to face. Great job with it!
Crazy scheme of course! Had a nice streak of luck early with very few twists, but couldn't get it together to win very quickly unfortunately.

column36: Kinda forgot about this for most of the week, so I allowed myself only one attempt. Wolverine was my first consideration due to how often wounds would come up, but then I noticed I didn't have much other means of KOing base cards. I also saw Photon for discarding and an abundance of X-Men. So I figured it was a fine time to bring in Angel, and Xavier would bring in a 3 attack for 2 along with KO potential. I was pleasantly surprised to find plenty of symmetry for Rogue, enough that I actually bothered to get Borrowed Strength.

Jessica Jones was nice to switcheroo up cards given that the HQ kept getting KO'd so quickly. I had thrown Blade and Jessica Jones in thinking the Marvel Knight heroes could potentially help avoid wounds from Master strike...but oddly enough, NONE came out! I'm not sure that has ever happened to me so that's a win in my book regardless of my score! lol.

Fun first game. Second was VERY rough. But the first one went well. We went with Angel and Ronin to help prevent the hero swing of 7 heroes, which our second game had. Luckily in the first game the right cards came out for both players to progress very well. Looking forward to the rest of the tourney.

column36:
Chose Core Black Widow for her Bystander saving and to pump up her attack cards that rely on bystanders. The attack cards did not factor in. Got one near the end of the game, but it didn't come out to play.

Chose Illuminati Beast for the dual-class cards to play into Agent Venom, Captain America 1941, and War Machine's cards that play into having different cards of different colors/classes. It didn't see a whole lot of play, but they did come in handy near the end of the game.

Red Guardian didn't see any action. My first two turns had him on the HQ, but they were wiped away from the scheme twists (figures). Only got one that was sent to Undercover right away. I was unable to find another card in the HQ that had a covert class so I can release him.

I would like to see this set up again, but all heroes must be SHIELD since most of the villains were Hydra affiliated.

Thanks for kicking this off with a great setup!  To counter the Anthems & Marches that General Thunderbolt Ross was attempting to drown us with, we zoned into our psychedelic trance mix so that we could foresee his moves and anticipate the coming twists.  Hellcat and Black Swan worked very well together for the P2 deck, with one copy of Dark Foretelling being enough to keep the twists manageable.  Hellcat added further vision to the villain and P2 deck, keeping things moving forward.  Meanwhile, P1 was more focused on building tech, strength and range combos with Captain Marvel, War Machine, Agent venom etc.  It was a good balance and allowed us to keep the city clean, manage helicopters well, and take down that Red Hulk before too many turns had gone by.  Second attempt using Bruce Banner/Hulkbuster was also fun, but was not quite as efficient.

games worked fine, theres probs better options in heroes than the ones i chose but i like them in combination with each other and captain marvel for a blue/green strat and its not often possible to mix them together.

lost the first turn because I got greedy healing a wound by playing an extra card from the VD and rescued Triage Nurse accelerating what was otherwise a really good game.

Second attempt went even smoother and won even faster although it came down to the wire due to Bagleshuffle TM all the twists into the first half of the VD. Super fun and Covert Investigating is a lot of fun.

There were a lot of hero choices available and a lot of mechanics to track, wife ended up sitting this one out. Still an awesome scheme but having to use some of least favorite Heroes did not endear me to the initial set up and definitely not to game too

This was pretty tough as far as narrowing down what 2 heroes to bring with such a wide choice of heroes. Game 1 went decent other than an unpreventable escape on turn 3 from cloning. Game 2 we decided to try a completely different strategy but almost ended up losing from scheme twists just from bad luck. The game went much faster and we ended up better off, but a lot of points were left on the board just from scheme twists and master strikes coming out instead of villains, henchman, and bystanders.
This scheme is very fun generally, good mix of heroes, Spider-Man 2099 came to play more than we expected round one

column36: Domino's uncommon turn 1 got me Venom's rare, which in turn lead to Proxima's and Domino's rares.

Nice thematic setup!  We thought Domino would be a great addition but unfortunately she was rarely in the GW long enough to recruit.  Player 1 used Wolverine and Blade to slice through the city and starting cards.  Player 2 leveraged Bishop and Magic.  Blade’s rare was clutch!

Always unique playing a game that has more than two villain groups and 7 total heroes. Having a little trouble getting out of the gate at game 1 as the Domino/Wolverine/Venom deck kept getting scheme twisted when we needed it most. 

However the true MVP game in round two when we had a working Iron Fist and Ghost Rider deck that was able to effectively hit Lilith every turn while we were able to dodge the Lillith villains and keep her bystander KO pile relatively small at 1 the entire game.

Great setup for week 1.  Got me back into the game after a while.  Using practically every bystander is nuts but has a wonderful sense of variety.  Loved starting with a setup that includes extra heroes and four half villain groups and two half henchman groups.  There was so many variables and so many keywords to play with and trigger.  Great start and I can’t wait for more this tournament.

Had one villain scoot out of the city on an unlucky Rise but it was Zombie Thanos and I was able to take care of him later.  Venom and Wolverine came together decently but at the end of the day the Killmonger-focused Strength deck almost single-handedly took out Lilith.

We choose Old Man Logan and Black Swan for their KO cards and again, the HQ often cycled past them faster than we could buy them. Unfortunately, Lilith's death metal mix was too much for us to handle.

The rock themed hero choices were inspired! Captain & the Devil has that 80's hair band look, Peter Parker is on the mic one of his cards and Hot Bowl of Soup must be the title of Aunt May's EP, so I feel like I stayed on theme. Deck 1 focused on 2-cost cards, and Jurassic America helped set off many triggers for Korg and Peter Parker. Deck 2 mostly skated by due to getting two copies of Elemental Guns out early so I had ample shards.

column36: The setup was a lot of fun and only real negative was how absolutely massive the 8 hero deck was. I'm happy with my hero choices. One player was Tech and Artifacts and the other was Strength. P2 got lucky with Thing's Rare and was able clean out the last 3 Alchemax Execs in one turn. Love big moments like that.

I saw the incredible amount of diversity in both villains and heroes, and I saw a lot of Green and Black. Surely a perfect setup for these two heroes specifically, right? Well in Game 1 they really came through and both heroes ended up saving me several times. In the game I'm reporting, I managed to recruit 3 of Spider-Gwen's "Save the Day" on one side and just went nuts. Spider-Gwen had a side with Ant Army and Star-Lord and ended up building 18 attack with 11 shards in the final turn, clearing both the city and the final tactic.

column36: Got a solid deck on one hand with Spiders and Ants. The other, slightly less effective deck was Thing with a tiny bit of Star-Lord support. Got pretty lucky with a long string of Tech cards in the Escape pile and not much else. Really decent game.

That game had so much randomness in it with the HQ our games were totally different in feel, even with the same heroes. And finally a quote from just before we started the game: "112 card hero deck. what is this bullsh*t?"

Attempt 2 went terribly, and I just didn't finish it. Attempt 1 went alright, albeit pretty slow and heavy. There were a lot of strong enemies in the CIty that kept me from focusing on the Masterminds. Eventually both decks got up to speed/strength and could deal with the Masterminds. Deck 2 focused mainly on Strength and Tech and Deck 1 Mostly on Thing and Captain Britain and a bit of Spider-Gwen.

This setup with all the random moments where HQ was emptied didn't feel like too much of a strategic way to deckbuild, mostly just down to luck. There was a bit too much recruit in the HQ for my taste, but not enough good cards to recruit from it. That's partly why it went so slow. I could prevent all escapes though, due to a lot of Scheme Twists early on and some Henchmen appearing as well. In attempt 2 only strong enemies appeared and I couldn't do anything about it, just shuffle luck I guess. It was fun to see that Killmonger was a good choice with the extra villain groups. This was definitely not my most fun game, but the first attempt was decent, so I'm giving it a 3/5. Curious to see what's up for next week's challenge.

column36: I will preface by saying I played my game on borrowed sleep since I work in a tax office and only managed to get this week's game in by sacrificing 3 hours of sleep. Despite my tiredness, I had fun. I regret choosing Phoenix for this setup because she KOs herself and I got  almost one scheme twist closer loss by her Koing herself 4 times. The all tech heros setup was nice for immunity to Ultrons Master Strike. One of my least favorite aspects was that more than half the game my city was menaced by the Future Ultron Prime who was harder to beat than Ulton mastermind due to so many tech cards in the HQ. Even selectively recruiting tech cards didn't help because the Scheme twist would wipe the HQ and it would come out mostly tech again. A little frustrating but overall fun.

I liked because we focused on a Spider-Man build that one of the players was able to get to cost cards every time a scheme twist came out. It just helped build the deck more rapidly. Our second hand did not have as much synchrony, but the characters in this grouping are kind of random… Or not, I guess they do all start with K.

Second game took a few more turns, but we got to use some of the other mechanics like outwit, which was a lot of fun. Also Killmonger specs can get pretty strong pretty quickly if you have the right card to play.

I had to add King Black Panther and Korg as the additional Heroes for the KPop Villain Hunters game. ;) Was fortunate that Henchmen came out early and I could thin my decks, and that the Master Strikes came later in the game. The Scheme Twist causing villains to shift city spaces, especially when moving out of a Time Incursion, also helped tremendously.

Player 1 scored Killmonge'sr rare early and Player 2 grabbed Kitty's rare. But both sides really profited mostly off dual class cards (strength and tech).  The 1 escape came early as well but after getting City under control , Kang went down 5 times pretty quick. Fun time.

column36: I think we did ok under the circumstances of this setup, and we developed our own synergistic decks even with 7 Heroes being used. Things evened out a bit once our decks started going, but there was still some heavy pressure from the villain deck all game. I don't usually like Schemes that KO non-grey heroes, but this is one of my favorite schemes. Pairing it with Galactus and Horsemen made it pretty difficult, but still fun.

column36:
Did not get many scheme twist so besides the timer it only contributed for providing the first bite of Galactus with some villains to feed on as well. Very strong villains in the beginning, so escapes were inevitable. 
Did not really think through hero choice, so I may have wasted Forge in the first week. X force wolverine actually turned out a good pick, his KO card I got first turn and helped in slimming one of the decks, while the other one was thinned by bishop and the various villain effects. Ended up with two 12 card decks in the end with only 4 grey cards in the first one and zero in the other. The latter deck I could consistently draw up in turns 18, 20, 22 and 24, providing Galactus with blue cosmic threats from Xavier and Bishop. 
So in the end, despite the escapes it was a quite quick win. 
(BTW after owning the game for more than a decade, I realized today that I played Burrow always as sending Moloids and company back to the sewers instead of the streets. )

Both my games were 28 turns wins surprisingly but felt quite different. I got really lucky that second time through. First time, I was one master strike or one scheme twist away from losing. Second time I was two of either away, def got luckier to draw less. Surprised I won twice, expected it to be harder tbh. Bishop is very strong. Brought Gambit and Emma to feed him cards, and can combo w/ the Prof X 2 cost common, so many X-men. Worked well, even with 7 heroes. I feel like I got pretty lucky in all, had a smooth game. Almost makes me want to rate the setup lower lol, went suspiciously smooth.

column2: When Yggdrasil Falls: A Quest in Nordic Punk
column36: Kylah started us off with a bang this season. The villains chosen to give more unavoidable hero KO's was diabolical. Hulk 2099 was crucial in putting wounds undercover and thinning the hero decks. Game 1 took longer than we would have liked, but managed to walk the fine line of barely managing the city. Left side went Beta Ray Bill with Hulkbuster, and right side played a Hulk 2099, Korg, Skaar mashup. Overall it was a decent start to the tournament, and looking forward to what's coming.

Both attempts started with both sides getting wounded twice. This led to unavoidable escapes. The twists came up at painful times, the hq was not cooperating. 2nd attempt was slightly slower. Hopefully Angel's bystanders will make up some ground.
Challenging but fun setup that felt rewarding to win.

Tricky setup! My first match went horrible and ended with twist 6 after 20 turns. I didn't take care enough of Hiroim (Warbound Villain). His fight effect KO's a card from the HQ and I did it this twice, because he came back into the city after one Strike. Suddenly I had 32 KO'd non-grey Heroes! 

In my first attempt I also realized that I needed a hero that can reveal the top card of the villain deck for the Uru-Weapons. So I swapped Forge with Punisher for attempt 2 and this was key for the win! 

Knowing WHEN to bury a twist deep down in the Villain Deck was crucial!! And although it took a while, the Magnificent Seven put Hela back to Asgard - finally! :-) :-) Kudos to Amadeus Cho and his strong attack!
This was a tough set up.   If it were not for very luck Uru weapons, I would have lost many turns earlier.    Losing all of the top cards out of the hero deck also felt bad.
I loved it! It was much better than cats! I'm going to see it again and again!

Things ended quite unfortunately for me, game one was a whiff, lasted 28 turns, only had one escape, but that escape caused me to lose on twist 6. Didn't really get to defeat much. Game two was shaping out much better, I had 2 escapes, 1 from the astral plane, and one from fenris wolf dashing in (which could've gone much worse), and by turn 24 I had two mastermind tactics down thanks to a strong left hand based around artifacts with a touch of smash. But because of the two escapes, and drawing twist 6 when the deck still had 13 other non twists available, it lost too soon. While it had more points by 12 even with being a shorter game, the worse round count and escape count led to me submitting game one. Very fun setup, the villains make a twist 6 defeat quite likely, so the clock is tight!

Yikes, that was rough and I totally suffered by not being familiar with this scheme (not in a set I own). The ways some of these villains KO’d non-grey heroes made for a rough go, and just couldn’t build deck synergy in either game. Tough to limp into the beginning of the tournament with an L.
